title: Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker Banned from EU Ports After AIS Spoofing Detection
summary: EU authorities imposed a ban on the Russian-flagged tanker *NS Century* for suspected sanctions evasion via AIS manipulation. The vessel remained tracked altering its position signals to obscure movements from Russian ports to India. This action highlights intensified maritime surveillance amid ongoing Ukraine-related sanctions.
contents:
# Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker Banned from EU Ports After AIS Spoofing Detection

## Overview
The European Union's maritime safety agency, EMSA, in coordination with member states, identified irregularities in the Automatic Identification System (AIS) data of the *NS Century*, a 15-year-old Aframax tanker owned by a shadowy Russian entity. Analysis revealed the ship had disabled its AIS transponder for over 48 hours while transiting the Baltic Sea, then reactivated it with falsified coordinates suggesting a legitimate voyage from Primorsk, Russia, to Paradip, India, carrying 80,000 tons of crude oil. This maneuver remains part of a broader pattern in the "shadow fleet"—over 600 aging tankers used by Russia to circumvent G7 and EU oil price caps set at $60 per barrel since 2022. EMSA's satellite AIS and long-range tracking corroborated the deception, linking the vessel to previous dark fleet operations. The ban, effective immediately, prohibits the tanker from entering any EU ports and mandates reporting by EU-flagged vessels. Industry trackers like Lloyd's List Intelligence noted this as the 17th such enforcement in 2026, with similar incidents involving Iranian and Venezuelan fleets. Russia denies violations, claiming technical glitches, but Western analysts predict further seizures as AI-enhanced AIS forensics improve detection rates by 40% year-over-year. (187 words)

## Location
- **Where**: Brussels, Belgium
- **Country**: België / Belgique / Belgien
